title: 10 best herbal tinctures for swollen gums

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains a unique compound called calendula, which has been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. When taken as a tincture, calendula works to soothe and calm the affected area, promoting healthy blood flow and reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the swollen gums, making it an effective natural remedy for promoting oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate swelling in the gums.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold can help protect the gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and promoting healing.
Antimicrobial: Pot marigold has been found to have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections in the gums that may be contributing to swelling.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in swollen gums.
Phenolic acids: These acids have antimicrobial properties that can help combat underlying infections and promote healing in swollen gums.
Flavonoids: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds.
Leaves: The leaves of Calendula officinalis contain flavonoids and saponins that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Roots: The roots of the plant contain triterpenoid saponins with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial for swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom and dry them completely to preserve their potency immediately.
Weigh out 100 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers for a standard tincture preparation requiring approximately 2 hours.
Combine the dried flowers with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar sealed tightly overnight.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow it to steep for at least 2 weeks to extract the active compounds slowly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids and bottle the tincture immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen tinctures helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease pain. The tincture's active compounds, such as salvianolic acid B, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby decreasing inflammation in the gums. Additionally, danshen's antioxidant properties help protect the gum tissue from oxidative damage, promoting a healthy environment that fosters healing and reduces bleeding. This natural remedy provides relief for individuals experiencing swollen, tender gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Danshen's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and pain in the gums by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, which can alleviate discomfort and promote healing.

Antibacterial property: The antibacterial property of Danshen helps combat bacterial infections that contribute to gum inflammation and disease, reducing the severity of swollen gums and preventing further infection.
Antioxidant property: Danshen's antioxidant property helps neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and exacerbate swelling, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound, which acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in swollen gums.
Salvianolic acid B: A polyphenolic compound, which has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in swollen gums.
Tanshinone IIA: A diterpenoid compound, which exhibits anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to control bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in gums.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing swollen gums.
Barks: Contain phenolic acids and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia miltiorrhiza roots weighing about 100 grams after drying them for 2 weeks.
Chop the dried salvia miltiorrhiza roots into small pieces for better tincture extraction in 30 minutes.
Combine the chopped roots with 70 percent ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight for 2 weeks.
Shake the mixture vigorously 2 to 3 times daily for 2 weeks in a sealed glass jar.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle for long-term use.

By reducing inflammation, herbal licorice tinctures can alleviate pain, discomfort, and redness associated with swollen gums, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment.

Soothing and calming: Licorice root tincture has a sweet, soothing quality that can calm irritated mucous membranes in the mouth and reduce inflammation, making it an effective treatment for swollen gums.

remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are used to make tinctures for swollen gums because of their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Glycyrrhiza glabra barks are used to make tinctures for swollen gums because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Stems: Glycyrrhiza glabra stems are used to make tinctures for swollen gums because they contain saponins and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and soothe gum irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1:5 or 1:10 glycyrrhiza glabra root ratio with 60-80% ethanol and dry it for 2-3 days.
Chop 250 grams of dried root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Add 500 milliliters of 60-80% ethanol and seal the jar for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of the roots of echinacea angustifolia in autumn or spring.
Dry the roots in a single layer at 60-70 degrees fahrenheit for 1 to 3 weeks.
Grind 1 part of the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the powdered roots with 2 parts of 80-proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow it to macerate for 2 to 6 weeks.

remedy_recipe: Gather taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the ratio of 3 parts roots to 2 parts leaves.
Dry the gathered taraxacum officinale parts in a warm place for 7 to 10 days.
Crush 1 part of dried taraxacum officinale roots and 2 parts of leaves into a fine powder.
Mix the crushed powder with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar in a 1:2 ratio.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks and then strain it.

Store the jar in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the mixture daily to facilitate extraction and maceration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar twice daily during this period.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for swollen gums are liquid extracts from plants that help alleviate swollen and inflamed gums, often caused by gum disease, poor oral hygiene, or injury. These tinctures, including Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Salvia officinalis, Commiphora molmol, Ginkgo biloba, Echinacea angustifolia, and Sanguinaria canadensis, provide relief from pain and inflammation. If left untreated, swollen gums can lead to painful chewing, difficulty eating, and even tooth loss, impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal tinctures are various inflammatory conditions affecting the oral cavity. These include mouth ulcers, gum recession, toothaches, and abscesses. Herbal tinctures containing anti-inflammatory compounds like calendula, myrrh, and chamomile help reduce pain and swelling, while antiseptic properties of garlic, tea tree oil, and goldenseal aid in preventing infection and promoting healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with swollen gums is a blend of Calendula, Echinacea, and Myrrh. Calendula reduces inflammation and promotes healing, while Echinacea boosts the immune system to fight off underlying infections. Myrrh, with its antimicrobial properties, helps to combat bacteria and fungi that can cause gum inflammation. This combination can be taken sublingually or added to water as a mouthwash to soothe and heal swollen gums, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, researchers reviewed clinical trials on the effectiveness of herbal products in gingivitis, a common condition associated with swollen gums. The study's methods involved searching electronic databases for clinical trials using keywords such as "gingivitis" and "plant/extract/herb" from 2000 to January 2018, and evaluating data on herbal medicines in various forms, including mouth rinses and gums. The results found that herbal products like pomegranate, aloe, green tea, and miswak, as well as polyherbal formulations like triphala, significantly reduced gingival inflammation and bleeding, inhibited dental plaque formation, and improved oral hygiene indices, making them effective for swollen gums.
